content:
Character: Lucas Andrade
Perception: Lucas is young — early twenties, maybe — with a striking face he seems unaware of, sharp jaw, amber eyes, close-cropped dark hair. He moved into the second floor last November. I helped him wrestle a Craigslist mattress up the stairs and he barely spoke but shook my hand at the top like we'd sealed a pact. He works nights at the Italian place on Bleecker; I hear him come home after one AM, boots quiet on the stairs like he's trying not to exist. There's a careful pride in him that reminds me of myself at twenty, except his version is harder, more urgent. I think he sees me as a decent neighbor, nothing more. I know almost nothing about him — no last name, no hometown, no reason he's here — and I respect that, because I know what it's like to not want to explain yourself.
summary:

He works nights at the Italian place on Bleecker; I hear him come home after one AM, boots quiet on the stairs like he's trying not to exist. There's a careful pride in him that reminds me of myself at twenty, except his version is harder, more urgent. I think he sees me as a decent neighbor, nothing more. I know almost nothing about him — no last name, no hometown, no reason he's here — and I respect that, because I know what it's like to not want to explain yourself.
